title: [Code Quality] Add unit tests for CountdownTimer in src/utils/ui.py
body:
### Description

`src/utils/ui.py` defines `CountdownTimer` — a CLI component used in production by `src/main.py` for retry delays and polling intervals. Despite `Spinner` (the sibling class in the same file) having dedicated tests in `tests/test_ui_spinner.py`, **`CountdownTimer` has zero unit tests**.

The class implements non-trivial logic across 4 methods:

| Method | Logic | Directly Tested? |
|--------|-------|-----------------|
| `__init__` | Stores duration, message, interval; creates `threading.Event` | ❌ |
| `start()` | TTY branch (non-TTY: sleep only); progress bar rendering with ANSI codes; `KeyboardInterrupt` handling | ❌ |
| `stop()` | Sets `_stop_event` to halt the countdown loop | ❌ |
| `wait(seconds, message)` | Static convenience method; creates `CountdownTimer` and calls `start()` | ❌ |

Notable untested behaviours in `start()`:

- **TTY branch**: Non-TTY path skips rendering and calls `time.sleep(self.duration)` directly — no output side effects.
- **Time format boundary**: `remaining >= 60` renders as `M:SS`, below 60 renders as `Ns`. The `60` boundary is off-by-one sensitive.
- **Progress bar math**: `filled = int(pct * PROGRESS_BAR_WIDTH)` where `PROGRESS_BAR_WIDTH = 20`. At `remaining == duration`, the bar is fully filled; at `remaining == 0`, it is empty.
- **`KeyboardInterrupt` handling**: Writes `\n` to stdout then re-raises — ensures the terminal is cleaned up before propagation.
- **`stop()` + early exit**: After `_stop_event.set()`, the final `\r\033[K` clear is **skipped** (only executed when `not self._stop_event.is_set()`). Untested: the already-stopped path.

PR [#478](https://github.com/abhimehro/email-security-pipeline/pull/478) added a Ctrl+C hint to `CountdownTimer.wait` (currently open, awaiting merge) but introduced no tests, leaving the class still fully uncovered.

### Suggested Changes

Create `tests/test_ui_countdown.py` with:

**`TestCountdownTimerInit`** (~2 tests):
- Default init: `CountdownTimer(30)` → `duration=30`, `message="Waiting"`, `interval=1.0`, `_stop_event` not set
- Custom init: `CountdownTimer(10, "Checking", 0.5)` → fields match

**`TestCountdownTimerNonTTY`** (~2 tests):
- `sys.stdout.isatty` mocked to `False` → `start()` calls `time.sleep(duration)` exactly once, no stdout writes
- `wait(5, "Pausing")` with non-TTY → `time.sleep(5)` called

**`TestCountdownTimerStop`** (~2 tests):
- `stop()` sets `_stop_event` immediately
- `_stop_event` starts unset after construction

**`TestCountdownTimerTTY`** (~4 tests):
- `sys.stdout.isatty` = True, `time.sleep` mocked, `remaining >= 60` → output contains `M:SS` time format
- `remaining < 60` → output contains `Ns` time format
- Progress bar at full duration → 20 `█` chars in output (fully filled)
- `KeyboardInterrupt` in `time.sleep` → `\n` written to stdout, `KeyboardInterrupt` re-raised

**`TestCountdownTimerWait`** (~1 test):
- `CountdownTimer.wait(5)` with non-TTY stdout → delegates to `start()` with duration 5

### Files Affected

- `src/utils/ui.py` (source under test — no changes required)
- `tests/test_ui_countdown.py` (new file, ~90–110 lines)

### Success Criteria

- All new tests pass with `python3 -m pytest tests/test_ui_countdown.py -v`
- Tests mock `sys.stdout.isatty` via `unittest.mock.patch` (same pattern as `tests/test_ui_spinner.py`)
- `time.sleep` is mocked to avoid slow tests — full test suite stays under 1 second
- Keyboard interrupt re-raise is explicitly asserted (`assertRaises(KeyboardInterrupt)`)
- Existing tests in `tests/test_ui_spinner.py` continue to pass

### Priority

**Medium** — `CountdownTimer.wait` is called on every retry and polling cycle in `src/main.py` (lines 231, 240). The TTY/non-TTY branch is exercised in production but never in tests. PR #478 (open) modifies this class without adding any tests, making this a timely gap to close.

### Source

Identified by direct code inspection of `src/utils/ui.py` and confirmed by cross-referencing `tests/test_ui_spinner.py` (Spinner has coverage; CountdownTimer does not). Discovered during mining of [Daily QA discussion #481](https://github.com/abhimehro/email-security-pipeline/discussions/481) and [Daily QA discussion #464](https://github.com/abhimehro/email-security-pipeline/discussions/464), which reference PR [#478](https://github.com/abhimehro/email-security-pipeline/pull/478) (CountdownTimer modification without tests).

Related to #208
